name: org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin
description: Maven plugin for launching the Machai MCP Server directly from a Maven build, exposing Machai AI tools through the Model Context Protocol over HTTP for local development, testing, demonstrations, and integration workflows without requiring a separate launcher. The plugin bridges Maven project context with the Machai MCP Server runtime: when a goal is invoked it resolves the project base directory and version from the active Maven project, constructs the appropriate server instance, registers available tools through Java SPI discovery, and starts the server on the configured port. It provides two HTTP transport modes as aggregator goals: mcp-server:stateless for independent stateless HTTP requests with no persistent session state, and mcp-server:streamable for incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection. Both modes expose the same SPI-discovered Machai tools, report server identity from the Maven project name and version, accept the same mcp.port configuration parameter, and manage the Jetty server lifecycle for the duration of the Maven goal execution.

usageContext: Start the Machai MCP Server from a Maven build with mcp-server:stateless or mcp-server:streamable goals.
Expose Machai AI tools over the Model Context Protocol via HTTP to MCP-compatible clients such as Claude Desktop.
Run stateless HTTP transport when each MCP request should be handled independently without persistent session state.
Run streamable HTTP transport when incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection is required.
Provide MCP tools with Maven project context including project base directory, project name, and project version.
Configure the HTTP server bind port with the required mcp.port parameter in plugin configuration or with -Dmcp.port on the command line.
Use Java SPI discovery through FunctionToolsLoader and GenericGenaiAdapter to register available Machai tools.
Manage a Jetty-backed MCP endpoint during local development, demonstrations, integration testing, or build-adjacent AI workflows.

constructors.description: Constructs the abstract base class for MCP server Maven plugin Mojos. The base class collects common parameters such as basedir, the active Maven project, and mcp.port for use by concrete server goals.

examples.description: Extend the shared abstract Mojo when implementing a custom Maven goal that needs the same MCP server configuration concepts as the built-in goals.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven Mojo for the mcp-server:stateless aggregator goal. Maven instantiates this class when the stateless goal is invoked, injects project context and the configured port, constructs an HttpStatelessMcpServer, registers tools, and listens until the process is stopped.

examples.description: Install and configure the ready-to-use plugin in pom.xml. Maven constructs org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.HttpStatelessServerMojo automatically when mcp-server:stateless is executed.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven Mojo for the mcp-server:streamable aggregator goal. Maven instantiates this class when the streamable goal is invoked, injects Maven project context and the configured port, constructs an HttpStreamableMcpServer, registers tools, and listens until the process is stopped.

examples.description: Install and configure the ready-to-use plugin in pom.xml. Maven constructs org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.HttpStreamableMcpServerMojo automatically when mcp-server:streamable is executed.

constructors.description: Creates a utility class for MCP server operations within Maven plugins. The package documentation identifies this class as providing utility functions for managing MCP server lifecycle.

examples.description: Instantiate the lifecycle utility directly in Java tests or embedded workflows that need MCP server management behavior.

features.name: mcp-server:stateless
features.description: Aggregator Maven goal that starts the Machai MCP Server in stateless HTTP mode. It constructs an HttpStatelessMcpServer, registers tools discovered through Java SPI, reports server identity from the active Maven project name and version, uses the Maven project base directory as server project context, binds to the configured mcp.port, and listens until the process is stopped. Stateless HTTP mode handles each request independently with no persistent session state.

examples.description: How to use it: run the stateless goal from a Maven project and supply the required port on the command line to expose a local MCP-compatible HTTP endpoint.
Invoke the stateless goal with fully qualified Maven plugin coordinates when the plugin prefix is not configured or an explicit version is preferred.

features.name: mcp-server:streamable
features.description: Aggregator Maven goal that starts the Machai MCP Server in streamable HTTP mode. It constructs an HttpStreamableMcpServer, registers the same SPI-discovered Machai tools as stateless mode, reports identity from Maven project name and version, uses the Maven project base directory as project context, binds to the configured mcp.port, and listens until stopped. Streamable HTTP mode supports incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection.

examples.description: How to use it: run the streamable goal from a Maven project and provide the required port when client workflows benefit from incremental result delivery over persistent HTTP transport.
Invoke the streamable goal with fully qualified Maven plugin coordinates for reproducible command-line usage.

features.name: MCP server lifecycle utilities
features.description: Provides utility functions for managing MCP server lifecycle operations within Maven plugins.

examples.description: Create the lifecycle utility in Java when testing or embedding MCP server management behavior outside normal Maven goal execution.

customizations.name: mcp.port
customizations.description: Required Maven plugin configuration parameter that defines the network port used by both the stateless and streamable HTTP MCP server goals. It can be supplied in the plugin configuration block or overridden with -Dmcp.port=... on the command line.

examples.description: Configure the HTTP port in pom.xml so developers can run mcp-server:stateless or mcp-server:streamable without repeating the port value on every command.
Override or provide the required port from the command line for a one-off stateless run.

customizations.name: basedir
customizations.description: Automatically resolved Maven project base directory. The plugin passes this directory to the server as the project directory so SPI-loaded tools can resolve project-relative paths during MCP interactions.

examples.description: Run the goal from the Maven project. The plugin resolves the active project base directory automatically and uses it as MCP server project context.

customizations.name: project name and version
customizations.description: Maven project metadata taken from the active project and reported in the MCP server identity block exposed to connecting clients.

examples.description: The active Maven project name and version are used by the plugin when identifying the running MCP server to clients.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.maven.AbstractMCPServerMojo
studs.description: Abstract base class for MCP server Maven plugin Mojos. The base class declares shared basedir, project, and mcp.port parameters used by concrete goal classes.

examples.description: Extend the abstract class when implementing another MCP-oriented Maven Mojo that should share the same configuration and parameter behavior.

examples.title: Install and run the stateless MCP server goal
examples.scenario: Start a stateless MCP-compatible HTTP endpoint from a Maven project for local development, integration testing, Claude Desktop integration, or demonstrations.
steps: Add org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to the build/plugins section of pom.xml or invoke it with fully qualified Maven coordinates.
Configure the required port in plugin configuration with <port>8080</port>, or pass it from the command line as -Dmcp.port=8080.
Run mvn mcp-server:stateless -Dmcp.port=8080 from the Maven project directory.
Connect an MCP-compatible client to the HTTP endpoint opened by the plugin-started server.
Use the exposed Machai tools during the development or test session, then stop the Maven process when finished.
examples.description: Practical installation, configuration, and execution flow for the ready-to-use stateless Maven goal. Stateless mode handles each HTTP request independently and is suitable for simple local MCP endpoint scenarios.

examples.title: Install and run the streamable MCP server goal
examples.scenario: Start a streamable HTTP MCP endpoint when a client workflow benefits from incremental result delivery over a persistent HTTP connection.
steps: Declare the plugin org.machanism.machai:mcp-server-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT in pom.xml or call it using fully qualified coordinates.
Set the required HTTP port with <port>8081</port> in plugin configuration or pass -Dmcp.port=8081 on the command line.
Run mvn mcp-server:streamable -Dmcp.port=8081 from the active Maven project.
Connect your MCP-compatible client to the streamable HTTP endpoint and use the same SPI-discovered Machai tools available in stateless mode.
When finished, stop the Maven process.
examples.description: Practical installation, configuration, and execution flow for the streamable Maven goal. Streamable mode supports incremental result delivery through streamable HTTP transport while still using Maven project context for server identity and project directory resolution.
